pattern: Counting one character and dividing by the length with integer (floor) division gives the rounded-down percentage in a single pass.
hints: You only care about how many times one specific character appears relative to the whole length.
Multiply the count by 100 before dividing so the floor happens at the right place.
Return (occurrences of letter) * 100 // len(s); integer division already floors toward zero for non-negative values.

edge_inputs.description: letter appears a fraction of the time, floors down
single character that does not match gives 0
exactly one third must floor to 33 not round to 34

stress.description: long random lowercase string with a chosen target letter
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    n = max(1, min(size, 100))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    letter = rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase)
    return [s, letter]
stress.note: letter often absent or rare so flooring edge cases appear
